title: Cat in the Hat Celebration Day!

The legendary Japanese right fielder capped his incredible career with a mind-boggling 4,367 base hits across Japan's NPB and America's MLB. Fans watched in awe as Ichiro's Seattle Mariners defeated the Oakland A's 5-4, marking the end of an era in baseball history on March 21.

story: Footsteps echoed on the pavement as Dr. Martin Luther King Jr. led a group of determined marchers out of Selma, Alabama. title: Comet Hale-Bopp's Dazzling Sky Show
story: Look up! A cosmic visitor is putting on a spectacular light show. Comet Hale-Bopp blazed across the night sky, reaching its closest point to Earth on March 22, 1997. title: Emmy Noether: Math's Hidden Hero
story: Emmy Noether wasn't your average mathematician. Born in 1882 on March 23, this German genius revolutionized abstract algebra. Despite facing challenges as a woman in science, Noether's brilliant mind couldn't be stopped. She developed groundbreaking theories that changed how we understand math and physics. Today, she's celebrated as one of history's most influential mathematicians, inspiring girls and boys alike to explore the exciting world of numbers.

story: Zoom! A spacecraft named Ranger 9 raced through space on a daring mission. Its target? The Moon! On March 24, 1965, it struck the lunar surface with pinpoint accuracy. title: Tree Huggers Save the Forest

It grew into a powerful way to protect forests across India.

title: Ancient Monarchy Ends in Ethiopia
story: A 3,000-year-old tradition came to an end in Ethiopia on March 21, 1975. The country decided to abolish its monarchy, which had ruled for thousands of years.
